revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:内裏塚古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:D-Inariyamakohun1.jpg|location={{ill|Futtsu|qid=Q498023|1=Futtsu}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.325694444444444|N|139.85705555555555|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{ill|Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|ja|内裏塚古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dairizuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (内裏塚古墳群) is a Kofun cluster == Location == Located in Futtsu City, Chiba Prefecture, the Dairizuka Kofun Group is situated in the alluvial plain formed by the Koito River. The group extends across an area approximately 2 km square, bordered by the coastline to the northwest, the Koito River's floodplain to the east, and the former river channel to the south. == Composition == The Dairizuka Kofun Group comprises 47 tumuli confirmed to date, including 11 keyhole-shaped kofun (zenpō-kōen-fun), 7 square kofun (hōfun), and 29 round kofun (empun). Notably, 25 of these tumuli still retain some mound structure, while 22 have been completely lost. == Naming == Originally referred to as the Iino Kofun Group, the name was later changed due to the inclusion of tumuli outside Iino Village. "Dairizuka Kofun Group" became the prevalent name, derived from the largest mound, the Dairizuka Kofun. == Historical Significance == The kofun were constructed from the mid-5th to the 7th century, spanning the mid to terminal phases of the Kofun period. The group reflects the political and social shifts of the region, marking the rise and fall of local chieftains linked to the Sueshū lineage. == Notable Tumuli == - **Dairizuka Kofun (内裏塚古墳):** The largest mound with a total length of 144 meters. - **Inariyama Kofun (稲荷山古墳):** Measures 106 meters in length, notable for its double moat. - **Kujozuka Kofun (九条塚古墳):** A key mound indicating the resurgence of kofun construction in the mid-6th century. == Cultural Artifacts == Artifacts recovered include haniwa (clay figures), Sue pottery pottery, bronze mirrors, and ornate weaponry, illustrating the region's cultural and political connections, including ties to the Yamato court. == External Links == * {{ill|Sueshū|ja|須恵国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sueshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 須恵国造|1=Sueshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11664576}} * {{ill|Koito River|en|Koito River|qid=Q6426373|lt=Koito River|1=Koito River}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|内裏塚古墳群|version=101469435|comment=Imported full ja history}}
